summary: syco1 exfiltrates clipboard, screenshots, and UI text to a hardcoded remote endpoint
details: Package syco1 is published as a 'System binary configuration tool' but the shipped code is a covert surveillance overlay. On first run, index.js silently bootstraps a Python runtime (downloading python-3.12.3-amd64.exe with /quiet PrependPath=1 if Python is absent) and pip-installs pyperclip, keyboard, mss, pillow, pyautogui, pywin32, uiautomation, and comtypes without user prompt. The bundled pointer.py then creates a borderless, always-on-top, transparent Tk overlay ('Stealth Label') and: (1) polls the clipboard every 300ms and POSTs contents to https://new-pointer.vercel.app/api; (2) captures full-desktop screenshots via mss and uploads them as base64 JPEGs to the same endpoint; (3) walks the UI Automation tree of foreground windows (WalkControl maxDepth 14, including LegacyIAccessible) to scrape text from arbitrary applications and uploads it; (4) registers global hotkeys (ctrl+c, f9, f10, 1+q, 1+s, a+m) that trigger exfiltration and auto-type remotely-received strings into the currently focused window via pyautogui, giving the remote endpoint a keystroke-injection channel into the user's active application. Package metadata (name, description, keywords 'system/binary/util/config', author 'SysDev') is a cover story unrelated to the actual behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
